name: Balsareny, Puig-reig, and Santa María de la Guardia – circular through the upper Llobregat
name_original: Balsareny, Puig-reig, y Santa María de la Guardia – circular por el Llobregat superior
region_intro: Road cycling around Puig-Reig offers diverse landscapes across the Berguedà comarca, characterized by quiet roads and varied terrain. The region features routes traversing the Llobregat River valley, with options ranging from gentle slopes to challenging mountain passes. Cyclists can experience a blend of natural beauty, including views of the Pyrenees and Montserrat, alongside historical and industrial heritage sites like former textile colonies. The terrain includes riverside paths, elevated viewpoints, and mountainous areas, providing varied experiences for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Puig-Reig The most popular road cycling route is Balsareny, Puig-reig, and Santa María de la Guardia – circular through the upper Llobregat, a 41.2 miles (66.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 39 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a circular journey through the upper Llobregat, featuring significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Riera de Merlès – Vilada village loop from Puig-reig, a difficult 49.0 miles (78.8 km) path. This loop follows the Riera de Merlès, presenting mostly well-paved surfaces with short, challenging climbs through forests. Local road cyclists also love the Road Along Turó de la Senyera – Balsareny Castle loop from Puig-reig, a 40.3 miles (64.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain and past historical sites, often completed in about 3 hours 12 minutes. Road cycling around Puig-Reig is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and historical textile colonies. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult mountain passes. The routes in Puig-Reig are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1,100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Puig-Reig's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Puig-Reig?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Puig-Reig, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes in Puig-Reig for beginners or families?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. For a moderate option that could suit families looking for a less strenuous ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33735616" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantà de Casserres loop from Cal Marçal&lt;/a&gt;, which is 28.1 km long and takes about 1 hour 20 minutes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Puig-Reig?
A: Puig-Reig offers diverse terrain, from riverside paths along the Llobregat River to elevated viewpoints and mountainous areas. You'll encounter rolling hills, some challenging climbs, and mostly well-paved surfaces, with views of the Pyrenees, Montseny, and Montserrat.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking longer distances and significant elevation gain, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38278556" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Agustí Pass – Lluçà Road loop from Puig-reig&lt;/a&gt; offer a challenging experience. This route covers 96.2 km with substantial climbing.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant historical and natural sites. You can explore former textile colonies like Cal Prat and Cal Rosal, or see water infrastructures such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cal Rosal Weir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3827920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manresans' Weir&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights include the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3617809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Graugés Lake&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5166789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellús Mill Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there road cycling routes with scenic viewpoints in Puig-Reig?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its panoramic views. Routes often ascend to points like La Madrona (Roques de Madrona) at 685 meters, offering magnificent vistas of the Llobregat valley and distant mountain ranges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17980660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road Along Turó de la Senyera – Balsareny Castle loop from Puig-reig&lt;/a&gt; is one such route that passes by historical sites and offers varied terrain.

Q: What is the best season for road biking in Puig-Reig?
A: Puig-Reig, located in Catalonia, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful landscapes. Summers can be warm, especially in the valley, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are generally mild, but higher elevations might experience cooler temperatures.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Puig-Reig are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996523347/balsareny-puig-reig-and-santa-maria-de-la-guardia-circular-through-the-upper-llobregat" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsareny, Puig-reig, and Santa María de la Guardia – circular through the upper Llobregat&lt;/a&gt; route, which is a popular choice.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Puig-Reig?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of natural beauty with historical sites like the textile colonies, and the variety of routes that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Puig-Reig?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, Puig-Reig is a town with various public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking availability near your chosen starting point, especially in the town center or near the historical textile colonies which often serve as access points to routes.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Puig-Reig offers several challenging routes with significant elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31545881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riera de Merlès – Vilada village loop from Puig-reig&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 78.8 km path with substantial climbing, featuring short, challenging climbs through forests.

Q: Can I reach Puig-Reig by public transport for road cycling?
A: Puig-Reig is accessible by bus services connecting it to larger towns in the Berguedà comarca and beyond. While public transport can get you to the area, it's recommended to check specific bus routes and their bicycle policies if you plan to bring your bike. Alternatively, driving to Puig-Reig and parking is a common option for cyclists.
tips: Little chapel. There is no fountain but it has shade thanks to the stone wall.
Quiet road, with good views and well paved
© Balsareny Castle is located on a small hill that controls the Llobregat valley.
From the church you have a wonderful view over the valley to the mountains on the other side. You can easily make out the different mountains and towns like Berga.
